violations.text: Pre-portioned Cole slaw was observed held at 45 degrees F. Inspector observed pan of portion cups was on top of ice, not submerged. PIC moved portion cups to refrigeration; options for temperature control discussed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2026

Alfredo sauce was observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit, in double-stacked plastic containers in the prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). Chloring sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC to call for service. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
A dishwasher was observed eating at the clean dish rack. Manager informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Another dishwasher was observed not washing their hands after carrying dirty dishes prior to putting gloves on. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils.PIC spoke with staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

A perforated pan was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Staff removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
The hand washing sink at the bar is lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (basement ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC stated ice machine is scheduled for regular maintenance; machine taken out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

Recently cooked and cooled lobster was observed held at 48 degrees F in the open prep unit; fish and shrimp were observed held at 45- and 44-degrees F in fry station cold hold; alfredo sauce was observed held at 45 degrees in the prep unit. Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All foods were brought to the walk-in to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
Working containers used for storing chemicals (hand soap) taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

Desserts re-packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025
Containers of food were observed stored on the floor in the basement walk-in. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
Hotel pans and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sinks in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the basement ice machines have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety licensed with RIDOH CFP. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
Staff observed filling buckets in hand sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
Hand sinks throughout the facility are lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Shellstock (oysters, clams) located in the walk-in did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items to be taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Cooked pasta was observed held at 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

Stuffies were observed held at 55 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep unit, recently prepared chicken salad was held at 59 degrees. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff moved foods to walk-in to rapidly chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
Containers of various foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezers. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of various seafood. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
Knifes were observed stored in gap between prep units between uses. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solutions for the bar dish machine and the 3-bay sinks. Inspector provided test strips during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. Inspector provided thermo labels during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/22/2024
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024
